seoTitle: How to Redesign Your Wix Website Without Losing Content
metaDescription: Learn how to modernize an existing Wix website using a staging clone, without losing your content, domain, or SEO setup.
intro: Redesigning a website you already rely on for traffic can feel risky, but Wix lets you clone your site as a staging copy so you can experiment safely. This guide covers duplicating your existing site, updating global design elements, and preserving critical integrations so your live traffic doesn't drop during the transition.

steps.heading: Duplicate your site
steps.body: Clone your existing website to create a staging sandbox you can safely redesign without affecting the live version.

steps.heading: Open the Site Design panel
steps.body: In your staging copy, open the global Site Design dashboard to update color themes and typography.

steps.heading: Update colors and fonts
steps.body: Refresh your color palette and re-map typography scales across the whole canvas.

steps.heading: Clean up section layouts
steps.body: Adjust structural section strips to fix spacing issues and realign old content blocks.

steps.heading: Replace outdated media
steps.body: Swap old images for fresh, high-resolution graphics throughout the site.

steps.heading: Preserve integrations and go live
steps.body: Double-check that forms, backend integrations, and SEO metadata carried over correctly, then route your domain to the updated site.

faq.question: Will redesigning my site hurt my SEO rankings?
faq.answer: It can if metadata or URLs aren't preserved carefully, so it's important to check your SEO settings survived the redesign before going live.

faq.question: Can I test the new design before my visitors see it?
faq.answer: Yes, working from a duplicated staging site lets you build and preview the redesign privately before publishing.

faq.question: Do my forms and backend integrations carry over automatically?
faq.answer: They typically carry over with a duplicated site, but it's still worth verifying each integration still works after the redesign.

faq.question: How do I switch my domain to the redesigned site?
faq.answer: Once you're satisfied with the staging version, you route your active custom domain to point at the updated site structure.
